title: Transition of the Cash Service Alliance (CSA) Web Application Access to the BSP Virtual Private Network (VPN)
text:
Reference No. ROSS-2026-03-0053
BANGKO SENTRAL NG PitlPINAS
OFFICE F THE DENrv GOVERNOR I REGIONAL OPERAnoNS AND ADVOCACY SECroR
MEMORANDUM No. 2026-013
For All Authorized Agent Banks (AABs)
Subject Transition of the Cash Service Alliance ItsA1 Web Application
Access to the BSP Virtual Private Network 01PN I
In line with the Bangko Sentral rig Pillpinas' (BSP) continuing frorts to
enhance operational esiliency and promote a more secure, reliable, an efficient
digital workspace. this is to inform all AABs that the BSP's Information Technology
Infrastructure and Op rations Department (ITIOD) is initiating the transition of the
CSA access from Citri to a VPN-based platform with a scheduled cutover date on
08 May 2026.
In view of the foregoing. AABs are hereby instructed to undertake necessary
actions and participate in the following activities to facilitate the smooth transition of
access to CSA from Citrix to VPN;
A PreparatoryActivities
I. AABs shall submit to the designated local system administrators of BSP
Regional Offices and Branches (ROBS). the updated and dulyaccomplished
VPN Regist ation Forms in PDF format riot later than 08 May 2026.
The approp iate VPN Registration Form to be accomplished shall depend
on the AAB's IT infrastructure capability. Accordingly, AABs are advised to
coordinate with their respective IT departments to determine the correct
VPN form t be completed.
Copies of t e forms are provided in the Remote VPN Form (Annex A1 and
Site-to-Site VPN Form (Annex B). Alternatively, the forms may be
downloaded from the following links:
a. Remote VPN Form
d. f
b. Site-to ite VPN Form
SI^^
Should there be any VPN concerns/queries and/or need for further
assistance our BSP IT Support may be reached through email at
2. AABs sha I assign responsible point persons to ensure efficient
coordinati n during enrollment, configuration. and testing activ'ties.
.
O 88.1. TBSP 0277) @ ESPmall@bsp. gov. ph @ bsp. gov. ph ^ A. Mahinl St. Malate 1004 Manila. Philippines
Classification: GENERAL

CSA Con, ^9,411;, bn and tosting
I. The IT departments of AAB Head Offices shall coordinate with their
respective ranch offices on the configuration and activation of their VPN
connectivit to ensure access to the CSA sites:
a. Primary Site
b, Second ry Site
2. AABs' authorized CSA users shall actively participate in the testing
activities that will be conducted by the ITIOD team,
3. During the parallel testing phase. AABs shall access CSA through both
Citrix and the CSA Web Portal via VPN. Given the use of two (2) portals,
intermittent access issues may be encountered. Nonetheless the BSP
assures that appropriate measures will be implemented to minimize
operational disruptions.
As part of the on tingency protocol, should VPN access issues arise, the AABs
shall be advised by BSP ROBS' local system administrators, upon confirmation from
IT100, to temporarily revert to Citrix access. Nori-compliance with the VPN on boarding
requirements by the cutover date may lead to loss of system access and may disrupt
operational processes dependent on the CSA ROB application.
The cooperat on and timely participation of all AABs are critical to the
successful implementation of this endeavor.
Any changes to the above activities will be communicated accordingly
Should there be further clarifications to the aforementioned activities. your
designated representative/s may coordinate with the designated local system
administrators of the BSP ROBS or via e-mail at
For guidance and appropriate action.
BERNADETrE -PUYAT
Deputy r r

2. The Registrant shall
. conform to all the technical requirements of the BSP;
. be responsible for any upgrades on hardware/software, configuration and troublesnooting to establish and
support the VPN connectivity. as may be required:
. Inform the BSP Business Department for
o the list of active IP addresses and authorized VPN users
o any changes in the VPN configuration five (5) days in advance to avoid disruption of the services.
3. The BSP may revoke, block, or permanently disallow the use of this service without prior notice due to reasons that
may compromise internal security.
4. Anyissues/errors encountered in the system, breach or compromise in the aforementioned application services shall

security breach and is ground for the termination of

REMINDERS

I. Application with incomplete/Inaccurate Information leg. , name of Participant user, IP address for Remote ACce^
VPN details. applicable Application Name, etc. .) will be returned to the Participant Institution.
2. The Participant Institution must accomplish one (1) VPN Connectivity Registration Form (Remote Access VPN) per
user
11. Guidelines
A Assistance may be requested from the IT Personnel (Network/Sealriq/) of the Participant Institution
accomplishing this section.
I. Requirements for Remote Access VPN Connectivity
a. Internet AccessService IMinimum 2Mbps)
b. Workstation
c. VPN client Ito be provided by BSP)
B. Complete the Remote Access VPN and'orTwo FactorAuthentication (2FAj details
. Only one device can be registered.
. For new VPN request, tick the "New VPN User"' and specify the New Device Information for 2FA.
. For replacement of device for2FA request, tick the ""Existing VPN User" and fillout the New and Retile
Device Information for2FA
. For reset of VPN password request, tick the "ResetVPN Password"
. For deletion of an existing VPN account request, tick the "Delete VPN Account'
C. Dormant or idle accounts for 90days shall be deactivated
D. If request for reactivation is not received, the account shall be deleted after being dormant for another thirty (30)
days from date of deactivation.
E. Requests for reactivetion of dormant accounts shall use the VPN Registration Form.
For further clarifications and inquiries, contact the concerned BSP Department
